disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Surrounding erythema blanches and is localised (1-2 cm rim); afebrile; no spreading redness/lymphangitis/induration/pus (against cellulitis)
trigger_question_examples: Does the red rim fade (blanch) when you press a glass on it, and is it spreading up the arm?
Any fever, red streaks up the arm, thick pus, or the skin very hot and hard?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The red rim fades when I press a glass, it's just around the plaques, no fever, no red streaks or pus, and it's not very hot.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No localised fluctuant tender swelling over the olecranon and painless passive joint movement (against septic bursitis/joint)
trigger_question_examples: Is there a soft, very tender swelling over the point of the elbow, and does moving the joint (not the skin) hurt deeply?
Can you bend and straighten the elbow without deep joint pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No soft tender lump over the elbow point, and moving the joint itself doesn't hurt deeply — it's the skin cracking.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Thick silvery-scaled plaques fissuring after lapsed topical therapy in dry weather
trigger_question_examples: Are these your known an autoimmune skin condition patches, and did you stop your cream?
Did the cracks start with the dry cold weather?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, my known an autoimmune skin condition patches; I stopped the cream weeks ago and the dry weather cracked them.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this infected and spreading? Do I need antibiotics or hospital?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain that a blanching, localised rim with no fever, pus, spreading streaks or hardness makes cellulitis unlikely; the pain/bleeding are dry fissures in thick scaly-plaque plaques. Advise thick emollient + dressings + restarting topical an autoimmune skin condition therapy, with a cellulitis/bursitis safety-net — without antibiotics/ED for an uninfected fissure.
